original: Jim Elliott (US) completes 24-hr paced outdoor race for 548.9 mi
China launches spacecraft Shenzhou 19 from Ganzu to the  Tiangong space station, with Wang Haoze, their first female space engineer and third female astronaut, on board
Walt Disney purchasesLucasfilmLtd, and its rights for Star Wars and Indiana Jones franchises, for $4.05 billion
NASA launches space vehicle S-203
22nd NASA Space Shuttle Mission (61-A): Challenger 9 launches
California AngelNolan Ryanthrows fastest recorded pitch (100.9 MPH)
Muhammad AliKOs George Foreman in the 8th round in "The Rumble in the Jungle" in Kinshasa, Zaire, regaining the world heavyweight boxing title with the famous "rope-a-dope" tactic
Emily Howell Warner, American pilot, 1st woman to captain a commercial American airline plane, born in Denver, Colorado (d. 2020)
Honduras and El Salvador sign a peace treaty ending the "Football War"
Smith Dairy at Orrville Ohio, makes largest milk shake (1,575.2 gal)
New DNA study of dogs suggests they were human's first domesticated animal, 11,000 years ago at end of the Bronze Age
P. T. Barnum's circus, "Greatest Show on Earth", debuts (New York City)
Branch RickeysignsJackie Robinsonto a contract with the Montreal Royals, the top minor league affiliate of the Brooklyn Dodgers
Brazilian McLaren driverAyrton Sennawins Japanese Grand Prix at Suzuka, his record 8th GP win of the season; clinches first Formula 1 World Drivers Championship

Stephen Schwartz's musical "Wicked", a re-telling of the "Wizard of Oz" from the Wicked Witch of the West's' point of view, starringIdina Menzeland Kristin Chenoweth, opens at the Gershwin Theatre, NYC; run is ongoing, wins 2 Tony Awards, 7 Drama Desk Awards, and a Grammy

Dutch Red Bull driverMax Verstappenwins the Mexican F1 Grand Prix in Mexico City for his record breaking 14th GP win of the season; surpasses Michael Schumacher and fellow German Sebastian Vettel (13)
Polina Astakhova, Soviet-Ukrainian gymnast (5 Olympic gold 1956, 60, 64), born in Zaporizhia, Ukrainian SSR, Soviet Union (d. 2005)
Leland H. Hartwell, American scientist (Nobel 2001 - discoveries of protein molecules that control the division (duplication) of cells), born in Los Angeles, California
Laura Nickel & Curt Noll find 25th Mersenne prime, 2 ^ 21701-1
Imtiaz Ahmed scores 209 v NZ, the record for a no 8 batsman
Panama 747SP lands after polar flight around Earth in record 54:07
A radio broadcast ofH. G. Wells' "TheWar of the Worlds," narrated by Orson Welles, allegedly causes mass panic
The Bosphorus Bridge in Istanbul, Turkey, is completed, connecting the continents of Europe and Asia over the Bosporus for the first time

Gerhard Domagk, German bacteriologist (Nobel Prize 1939 in Physiology or Medicine - first commercially available antibiotic and marketed under the brand name Prontosil), born in Lagow, Brandenburg (d. 1964)
Daniel Nathans, American microbiologist (Nobel 1978 - discovery of restriction enzymes and their application in restriction mapping), born in Wilmington, Delaware (d. 1999)
Sandra Magnus, American engineer and NASA astronaut (STS-119 Discovery), born in Belleville, Illinois
NY Daily News runs headline "Ford to City: Drop Dead"
Grace Slick, American rock vocalist (Jefferson Airplane/Starship - "White Rabbit", "Somebody to Love"), born in Highland Park, Illinois
John J Loud patents ballpoint pen
NASA launches Flt Satcom-4
WWF conservation group says "exploding human consumption" has caused a 60% decline in all wildlife between 1970 and 2014
Amy Beach's "Gaelic Symphony" debuts by the Boston Symphony Orchestra; the first major work by an American female composer receives "public and journalistic acclaim."

FIFA Ballon d'Or: Argentina and Inter Miami CF forwardLionel Messiwins his record 8th award from Manchester City strikerErling Haaland; Spanish World Cup MVP Aitana Bonmati takes out the women's award
Robert L. "Hoot" Gibson, American aviator, aeronautical engineer and astronaut (STS 41B,61C,27,47,71), born in Cooperstown, New York
Gustavus Adolphus becomes King of Sweden at 17 (1611-32)
USSR Kosmos 186 & 188 make 1st automatic docking & Venmera 13 launch
In Japan, NEC releases the first 16-bit home entertainment system, the TurboGrafx-16, known as PC Engine.
Britain and France complete the "Chunnel" under the English Channel
Oakland Raiders break the single game record for most penalties in an NFL game gaining 23 in win over Tampa Bay Buccaneers
Tamla/Motown releasesStevie Wonder's documentary film soundtrack "Journey Through The Secret Life of Plants"
US PresidentJoe Bidensigns the country's first AI executive order, with directives for US federal agencies use of AI
Napoléon Bonaparte admitted to the elite École Militaire in Paris, the start of his military career
Patsy Montana [Ruby Rose Blevins], American country singer, songwriter and yodeler ("I Want To Be A Cowboy's Sweetheart"), born in Beaudry, Arkansas (d. 1996)
Otis Williams [né Miles], American baritone R&B and pop singer and songwriter (The Temptations - "Just My Imagination"), born in Texarkana, Texas
German U boat fails attack of English battleship Nelson withWinston Churchill, Dudley Pound & Charles Forbes aboard
English metal band Black Sabbath make US concert debut at  Glassboro State College in Glassboro, New Jersey; show interrupted twice when they blow out the power
Richard Arrington, Jr. is elected the first African American mayor in Birmingham, Alabama
Clarence Birdseyesells first frozen peas
Nobel prize for chemistry awarded to Lars Onsager (thermodynamics)
Nobel prize for physics awarded to Luis Alvarez (bubble chamber)
Polish tennis star Agnieszka Radwańska wins the WTA Finals title at Kallang. Singapore; beats CzechPetra Kvitová6–2, 4–6, 6–3
Thomas Nicely reports bug inIntel's Pentium-processor on the Internet
Czech tennis starPetra KvitovábeatsVictoria Azarenkaof Belarus 7–5, 4–6, 6–3 in the WTA Championship decider in Istanbul, Turkey
Former basketball playerMagic Johnsonis the fourth athlete to become a billionaire (after Michael Jordan,Lebron James, andTiger Woods), according to Forbes
Dickinson W. Richards, American physician (Nobel 1956 - development of cardiac catheterization and the characterisation of a number of cardiac diseases), born in Orange, New Jersey (d. 1973)
Leon Day, American Baseball HOF pitcher (9 x NgL All Star; NgL World Series 1946; Brooklyn/Newark Eagles, Baltimore Elite Giants), born in Alexandria, Virginia (d. 1995)
Clifford Brown, American jazz trumpeter and composer (Joyspring, Jordu), born in Wilmington, Delaware (d. 1956)
Aleksandr Lazutkin, Russian cosmonaut (Soyuz TM-25), born in Moscow, Russia
Queen Isabella of Spain bans violence against Indians in the Americas
First public railway in the Russian Empire opens - the Tsarskoye Selo Railway, running 27 km from Saint Petersburg to Pavlovsk
